help_text.vpn_mode_general: All traffic through VPN, except exclusions
help_text.vpn_mode_selective: All traffic direct, except specified routes
help_text.uninstall_description: Completely remove TrustTunnel from the server: stop the service, delete files and configuration.
help_text.uninstall_consequences: The service will be stopped, all TrustTunnel files will be deleted from the server. The current VPN connection will be terminated.

tooltips.kill_switch_detailed: When the VPN connection drops, all internet traffic is blocked to prevent your data from leaking outside the VPN. Recommended to keep enabled for maximum security.
tooltips.anti_dpi_detailed: Disguises VPN traffic as regular HTTPS to bypass Deep Packet Inspection (DPI) systems used by ISPs and government agencies to block VPNs.
tooltips.post_quantum_detailed: Uses encryption algorithms resistant to quantum computer attacks. Protects your data from future decryption. Slightly increases connection time.
tooltips.ipv6_detailed: Allows IPv6 traffic routing through the VPN tunnel. If your ISP or server doesn't support IPv6, keep this disabled to avoid connection issues.
tooltips.dns_upstreams: Custom DNS servers for queries through VPN. Multiple servers work as fallbacks: if the first doesn't respond, the next is used. Encrypted protocols DoT, DoH, DoQ are supported to protect DNS queries from ISP interception.
tooltips.mtu: MTU size (576–9000). Lower values are more reliable on poor connections, higher values improve throughput.

tunnel.title: Tunnel
tunnel.socks_auth_hint: SOCKS5 proxy authentication. Leave empty if the proxy is only for you.

security.title: Security
security.custom_sni_description: Masks TLS connection as another domain to bypass DPI. Leave empty for standard SNI.
security.custom_sni_warning: With Let's Encrypt certificate, custom SNI may cause connection failure — certificate won't match the spoofed domain.

experimental.title: Experimental Features
experimental.description: Features under development. May be unstable.

app.start_minimized_desc: Hide window on launch, show only tray icon
app.auto_connect_desc: Connect to VPN automatically on startup
app.logging_desc: Save application events and VPN engine output to files

messages.config_required: Please select a configuration file or set up a server first
messages.coming_soon: In development — will be available in future updates
messages.vpn_not_installed_install_prompt: TrustTunnel is not installed on this server. Would you like to install it?
messages.domain_already_exists: Domain "{{domain}}" is already in the list
messages.domain_in_ru_list: Domain "{{domain}}" is already included in RU domains

errors.internet_lost_disconnecting: Internet connection lost. Disconnecting VPN, waiting for network recovery...
errors.network_recovery_timeout: Network recovery timed out (5 min). Please reconnect manually.
errors.auth_required: Authorization error: invalid username or password. Update config from server via Control Panel.
errors.wintun_missing: Failed to create VPN adapter. Run the application as administrator.
errors.listener_failed: Failed to start VPN tunnel. Check administrator rights and wintun.dll presence.
errors.connection_refused: Server refused connection. Check if VPN service is running on the server.
errors.adapter_timeout: VPN adapter creation timed out. Restart the application as administrator.
errors.adapter_conflict: Detected VPN adapters from other software: {{adapters}}. This may cause connection issues. Consider disabling them.
errors.panelCrash: An error occurred in the {{panel}} panel. Other panels still work.

confirmations.irreversible_action: Are you sure? This action is irreversible.

updates.current_version_latest: You have the latest version installed

welcome.description: Set up a server in minutes or use an existing config.
welcome.setup_description: Deploy VPN with HTTP/2, HTTP/3, QUIC, TCP, UDP, ICMP tunneling and Kill Switch on your own server
welcome.fetch_description: Connect to an already configured server and download your VPN configuration
welcome.skip_description: Load an existing .toml configuration file from disk
welcome.import_description: Import config from file, link, or clipboard
welcome.requirement_linux: • Linux server (Ubuntu 22+, Debian 11+) with SSH access
welcome.requirement_resources: • Minimum 1 CPU core and 512 MB RAM (1 GB recommended)
welcome.requirement_root_domain: • Root access • Domain pointed to server IP

server.title: Server Connection
server.description: SSH credentials to access your server
server.host_hint: IP address or domain name of your VPS

checking.title: Checking server...
checking.description: Connecting via SSH and checking TrustTunnel installation

found.select_user_for_config: Select a user to save config
found.no_users_description: TrustTunnel is installed on the server, but no users are configured. Set up the server to add users.
found.not_installed_fetch_description: TrustTunnel was not found on the server. Set up the server first.
found.ssh_error_fetch_description: Could not connect to the server via SSH. Check your connection details.
found.add_user_description: Each device should connect with its own user.
found.user_already_exists: User with this name already exists
found.confirm_delete_message: User "{{user}}" will be removed from the server. Their configuration will stop working.
found.connection_error_help: Could not connect to the server via SSH. Check the address, port, username and password. The server may be unreachable from your network.
found.host_key_reset_help: The server was likely reinstalled. Go back and connect again.
found.not_found_can_install: TrustTunnel not found — can install from scratch
found.uninstall_consequences: The service will be stopped, all TrustTunnel files will be deleted from the server. The current VPN connection will be terminated.

endpoint.title: VPN Settings
endpoint.self_signed_warning: Self-signed certificates are insecure and may be blocked. We strongly recommend using a domain with Let's Encrypt.
endpoint.dns_record_help: A record for domain → {{host}}, port 80 open
endpoint.dns_record_help_no_host: A record for domain → Server IP, port 80 open
endpoint.dns_warning_text: make sure the A record for domain {{domain}} points to IP {{host}}. Without this, Let's Encrypt cannot issue a certificate.
endpoint.dns_warning_text_no_host: make sure the A record for domain {{domain}} points to your server IP. Without this, Let's Encrypt cannot issue a certificate.
endpoint.system_requirements: Requirements: Linux x86_64, 512 MB RAM, 100 MB disk, port 443
endpoint.cert_chain_path_help: Full path to cert.pem file on the server
endpoint.cert_key_path_help: Full path to key.pem file on the server

deploying.title: Installing TrustTunnel...
deploying.description: This may take a few minutes

done.title: All done!
done.fetch_description: Configuration fetched from server and saved.
done.deploy_description: Server installed and running. Configuration created automatically.

error.title: Something went wrong
error.hint_dns: The DNS record (A record) for the domain does not point to the server IP address. Add an A record in your domain control panel and wait 5–10 minutes.
error.hint_letsencrypt: Let's Encrypt could not issue a certificate. Make sure the domain points to the server and port 80 is open.
error.hint_port_80: Port 80 must be open and not occupied by another service (nginx, apache) to obtain a certificate.
error.hint_unreachable: Server unreachable. Verify the IP address is correct, the server is running and not blocking connections from your network.
error.hint_auth_failed: SSH authentication error. Check your username and password.
error.reinstall_prompt: Would you like to reinstall VPN on the server?
error.reinstall_description: Config fetching failed again. You can reinstall VPN from scratch.

import.title: Import Configuration
import.from_file_desc: Select a .toml configuration file from disk
import.from_clipboard_desc: Paste config or link from clipboard
import.clipboard_invalid: No TrustTunnel config or link found in clipboard

routing.domains_will_auto_load: They will be automatically loaded into config after setting up a connection.
routing.list_changed_apply_on_connect: List changed — will apply on connect
routing.mode_selective_description: Mode: Direct. Domains below will go through VPN, all other traffic — direct.
routing.mode_general_description: Mode: Everything via VPN. Domains below will go direct, all other traffic — through VPN.
routing.directDescription: Traffic to these addresses will bypass the VPN tunnel.
routing.proxyDescription: Traffic to these addresses will be routed through the VPN tunnel.
routing.blockDescription: Traffic to these addresses will be blocked.
routing.redundantHint: This is already the default behavior in the current mode. Entries are saved and will apply when the mode is switched.

validation.invalidCyrillic: Cyrillic domain must include a dot (e.g. сайт.рф)
validation.invalidFormat: Allowed: domain, IP, CIDR, geoip:, geosite:

routing.geodataDescription: GeoIP and GeoSite databases for country and category-based routing.
routing.processFilterDescription: Manage VPN routing for specific applications.
routing.processExcludeDescription: Selected processes will bypass the VPN.
routing.processOnlyDescription: Only selected processes will use the VPN.
routing.reconnectMessage: Routing rules updated. Reconnect VPN to apply changes?

about.description: VPN client for the TrustTunnel protocol. Deploy your own VPN server in one click, manage it remotely, and connect with traffic indistinguishable from regular HTTPS.
about.vibe_coding: Built with vibe coding — an AI assistant writes code based on natural language task descriptions.
about.up_to_date: You are running the latest version

dashboard.speed_test_failed: Speed test failed. Try again later.
dashboard.connect_to_see: Connect to VPN to see monitoring data
dashboard.server_stats_hint: Connect via Control Panel to see server stats

status.title: Protocol Status
status.rebooting_desc: Waiting for connection to restore. This usually takes 30–60 seconds.
status.check_ssh: Check SSH credentials and server availability
status.not_installed_desc: Server {{host}} is reachable, but TrustTunnel was not found.
status.tooltip: TrustTunnel status on the remote server. You can restart the service or reboot the server from here.

version.title: Version & Updates
version.tooltip: Manage TrustTunnel version on the server. Upgrade to latest or install a specific version.
version.install_wip: In development. Will be available in future updates.
version.confirm_message: TrustTunnel {{version}} will be installed. The VPN service will be briefly restarted.
version.confirm_message_downgrade: ⚠ You are installing an OLDER version ({{version}}). Configuration may be incompatible. The VPN service will be restarted.

users.title: Users
users.confirm_delete_message: User "{{user}}" will be removed from the server. Their configuration will stop working.
users.tooltip: VPN client accounts. Each device should connect with its own user.

logs.title: Server Logs
logs.tooltip: Last 100 lines of TrustTunnel logs. Useful for troubleshooting.

diagnostics.title: Diagnostics
diagnostics.tooltip: Automatic server health check: network, service, configuration, certificates.

config.title: Server Configuration
config.tooltip: Current server settings from vpn.toml. Read-only view.

cert.title: TLS Certificate
cert.tooltip: Information about the TLS certificate used for VPN connections.
cert.renew_confirm_message: The TLS certificate renewal will be attempted. This may take some time.
cert.renew_wip: In development. Will be available in future updates.
cert.error_certbot_failed: Certificate renewal failed. Check server logs for details.
cert.error_rate_limit: Certificate was recently renewed. Let's Encrypt limits renewals — try again later.

export.title: Export Configuration
export.tooltip: Generate config for mobile or desktop clients. Deeplink format supports QR codes.
export.description: Generate a QR code or deeplink for quick connection from mobile or desktop clients.

security.title: Security
security.tooltip: Protect your server with fail2ban (brute-force protection) and UFW firewall.

fail2ban.title: Fail2ban
fail2ban.desc: Blocks IPs after repeated failed authentication attempts.

firewall.title: Firewall (UFW)
firewall.desc: Controls which ports accept incoming connections.
firewall.warn_lockout: SSH port {{port}} will be allowed before enabling — your current connection is safe.

errors.port_invalid: Invalid port — use a number (e.g. 443) or range (e.g. 80:90)
errors.port_range_invalid: Port range must be start:end with start < end
errors.source_invalid: Invalid source — use any, IPv4/IPv6 address, or CIDR (e.g. 10.0.0.0/24)
errors.generic_rule_rejected: Rule rejected — check port, source and comment

confirm.install_fail2ban_message: fail2ban will be installed via apt and configured with a default sshd jail (5 retries, 1h ban).
confirm.uninstall_fail2ban_message: fail2ban will be stopped and the package completely purged from the server.
confirm.stop_fail2ban_message: The service will be stopped but the package stays installed. You can start it again anytime.
confirm.install_firewall_message: UFW will be installed and enabled. SSH port {{ssh}}, VPN port {{vpn}} (tcp+udp){{http}} will be opened. Default policy: deny incoming.
confirm.uninstall_firewall_message: UFW will be disabled and the package completely purged. All rules will be lost.
confirm.stop_firewall_message: UFW will be disabled but the package and all rules remain. You can re-enable it anytime with the same rules intact.
confirm.delete_rule_message: Rule #{{n}} will be removed from the firewall.

danger.title: Danger Zone
danger.tooltip: Actions that may cause data loss or VPN disconnection.
danger.confirm_reboot_message: The server will be completely rebooted. VPN connection will be interrupted during reboot.
danger.confirm_uninstall_message: The service will be stopped, all TrustTunnel files will be deleted from the server. The current VPN connection will be interrupted.

control.ssh_description: Enter SSH credentials to manage server

sshErrors.timeout: Could not connect to {{target}} — connection timed out (15s)
sshErrors.hostKeyChanged: Server host key has changed! This could indicate a man-in-the-middle attack, or the server was reinstalled. Clear the host key in Server settings and try again.
sshErrors.hostKeyReset: Host key was reset. Press Connect again.
sshErrors.hostKeyResetHelp: The server was likely reinstalled. Go back and connect again.
sshErrors.addUserFailed: Failed to add user to credentials.toml
sshErrors.deleteUserFailed: Failed to delete user from credentials.toml
sshErrors.configCreateFailed: Failed to create configuration files. Check logs.
sshErrors.readConfigFailed: Failed to read vpn.toml on the server
sshErrors.exportFailed: Failed to export config (code {{code}}). Check credentials.toml on the server.
sshErrors.exportFailedUsers: Failed to export config (code {{code}}). Available users: {{users}}
sshErrors.deeplinkExportFailed: Failed to export deeplink (code {{code}}). Check username '{{user}}'.
sshErrors.uninstallFailed: Could not fully uninstall TrustTunnel (code {{code}}). Check logs.
sshErrors.certRenewFailed: Failed to renew certificate (code {{code}}). Check logs.
